title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: George Schubert, 81, and his dog were killed in an early morning house fire in Foxborough.
The fire occurred on Granite Street around 3 a.m., and the Foxborough Fire Department responded promptly.
The cause of the fire is under investigation despite the house sustaining significant damage.

title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's tree canopy expanded by 151 acres from 2019 to 2024, increasing coverage to 28.5%, a 0.5% rise.
East Boston, Jamaica Plain, South Boston, and West Roxbury saw the largest canopy gains; Hyde Park and Mattapan had losses.
The expansion aligns with Boston's climate goals, supported by the 2022 Urban Forest Plan to enhance tree planting initiatives.

title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Robert Kraft's group agrees to help cover $7.8 million in World Cup security funding for Gillette Stadium events.
Foxborough officials remain unsatisfied with current proposals, citing unresolved concerns about event security funding and arrangements.
Select Board sets March 17 deadline to decide on granting the event license for the seven World Cup games.

title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Saints Tavern in Fenway, operating for nearly a decade, is closing due to rising insurance costs.
The bar was a popular spot for Northeastern students and made 85% of sales from liquor.
Insurance hikes saw costs jump from $18,000 to over $75,000, prompting the bar's closure on March 9.

title: 4 questions facing Celtics ahead of Jayson Tatum’s expected return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum is expected to return for the Boston Celtics against the Dallas Mavericks following a 10-month absence.
Tatum's return raises questions about lineup adjustments and workload management under head coach Joe Mazzulla.
Celtics may face roster shifts impacting players like Baylor Scheierman, due to Tatum's integration back into the team.

title: How did Jayson Tatum’s Achilles’ tendon recovery unfold? Recapping the Celtics star’s journey.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum's Achilles' tendon surgery occurred on May 13, 2025, performed by Dr. Martin O'Malley in New York City.
Tatum's recovery milestones include attending a Patriots' practice without a walking boot and participating in G-League practices.
The Celtics updated Tatum's status to questionable, with a potential return against the Dallas Mavericks on March 6, 2026.

title: Choosing which of my two cars to keep
author: John Paul
bullets: AAA Northeast's Car Doctor advises reader on keeping 2005 or 2007 Honda Element based on condition and features.
Assessment of factors includes rust, mechanical health, and additional safety features like side airbags in the 2007 model.
Older Honda Elements have cult status, potentially affecting their value and desirability in the used car market.

title: Patriots host former top-10 draft pick for free-agent visit
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots hosted free agent offensive tackle Jedrick Wills, formerly with the Cleveland Browns, for a meeting.
Wills, drafted 10th overall in 2020, started 57 games for Browns but missed 2025 season due to injury.
With backup tackles as free agents, Patriots assess potential additions despite committing to Will Campbell as starting left tackle.

title: RFK Jr. responds to uproar after singling out Dunkin’ for sugary drinks
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. criticized Dunkin' and Starbucks for beverages with high sugar content at a Texas health rally.
Governor Maura Healey defended Dunkin', sparking a debate that split Boston.com readers nearly evenly on the issue.
Kennedy clarified that his focus is on sugar content in drinks, not a specific attack on Dunkin' or its products.

title: Patriots sign veteran edge rusher to 1-year contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots sign Jesse Luketa to a one-year contract to strengthen their pass-rushing depth.
Luketa, 27, played 31 NFL games with 31 tackles, three sacks, and one forced fumble.
He spent the 2025 season on the Rams' practice squad after recovering from a thigh injury.
